body.title: Inference loop: replace blind identical-retry on availability-exhaust with honest ESCALATE
body.intention: I intend that when a build hits an availability or timeout wall the system escalates honestly instead of silently re-running the identical doomed path and burning wall-clock.
body.description:
**Problem:** on an inference availability/timeout error at difficulty 'code', the domain select loop retries the IDENTICAL growing-context path (retry 1/2, then 2/2). In the 2026-07-03 observe-run this re-ran the same doomed 39-turn loop THREE times (~11 min each, ~34 min total) before HALT 'inference-availability-exhausted' — pure wasted wall-clock, because nothing about the request changed between attempts.
**Proposed shape:** on availability-exhaust, do NOT blind-retry the identical request. Either ESCALATE honestly (surface the reason, hand off) OR retry only with a CHANGED strategy (e.g. after context eviction from T-agentic-loop-context-discipline, or a different model/tier). Verify the exact retry site (likely unseen_university/devices/inference/domains/base.py select/retry loop).
**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/domains/base.py (the availability retry/select loop — confirm exact site).
**Design rules:** CP1 (I-don't-know / honest escalate over false persistence); inference-cost-optimizing-router.
**Scope boundary:** IN — replace blind identical-retry with honest ESCALATE or a changed-strategy retry on availability-exhaust. OUT — the num_ctx setting and context eviction themselves (their own tickets).
**Test plan:** unit — simulate repeated availability errors; assert the loop escalates (or changes strategy) rather than re-issuing the identical request N times.
**Completion criteria:** no identical-retry path remains; an availability-exhausted build escalates with a named reason (or retries only with a changed request).
**Rollback:** git revert.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)

[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)

[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
